title: Reproducible environment recipe + CI harness that runs a tutorial
context: Cancer tools are notoriously version-sensitive, and tutorials that do not pin their environment become broken within months. This project requires every tutorial to ship a pinned environment (lockfile + optional container digest) and a CI workflow that recreates the environment and runs the tutorial end-to-end on shipped/synthetic data. Without this harness, 'CI green' is meaningless — linting without execution is not reproducibility. This task depends on template-003 so the environment-pin block in templates is consistent with the harness.
objective: Build and commit a reproducible-environment recipe and CI harness that, given a tutorial's lockfile, recreates the environment on a clean machine and executes the tutorial end-to-end headlessly on shipped/synthetic data, recording the run reference in reproEnv.ciRunRef.
acceptanceCriteria: Provides a pinned-environment recipe per ecosystem (conda-lock/pixi/renv.lock/hashed requirements) plus an optional container digest; 'reproducible' means a clean machine given only the lockfile runs the artifact to completion with recorded outputs.
A CI workflow recreates the environment and executes one tutorial/test end-to-end headlessly on shipped/synthetic data; the run reference is recorded in reproEnv.ciRunRef.
The harness code is MIT-licensed; pnpm build && pnpm test && pnpm lint are green for any Elyos-side tooling.
At least one tutorial runs successfully through the harness end-to-end, demonstrating the approach works before the pilot contribution (pilot-007) depends on it.
resources: conda-lock documentation; pixi documentation; renv documentation; GitHub Actions documentation
output: A committed reproducible-environment harness (lockfile recipe + CI workflow) MIT-licensed, with evidence of at least one tutorial running end-to-end on shipped/synthetic data, enabling all subsequent tutorial and test contributions to claim genuine reproducibility.
